name: Urederra Waterfall and Balcon de Pilatos Viewpoint – Urbasa-Andia Natural Park
name_original: Cascada de Urederra y Mirador del Balcón de Pilatos – Parque Natural de Urbasa-Andía
region_intro: Hiking around Monte Común De Las Améscoas offers diverse natural landscapes within the southwest of the Urbasa-Andía Natural Park in Navarra, Spain. The region features a karst topography with cliffs and ravines, alongside extensive beech forests and mountain pastures. Hydrological features include the Nacedero del Urederra, a significant natural monument. This area presents varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Monte Común De Las Améscoas The most popular hiking route is Urederra Waterfall and Balcon de Pilatos Viewpoint – Urbasa-Andia Natural Park, a 9.8 miles (15.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 38 minutes to complete. This route explores the iconic turquoise pools and cascading waterfalls of the Urederra River source. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cave of the Cristinos - Hermitage of San Benito – circular from Larraona, a moderate 3.9 miles (6.3 km) path. This trail leads through varied terrain, passing by the Cave of the Cristinos and the Hermitage of San Benito. Local hikers also love the Ubaba Viewpoint – Arteko Saro Dolmen loop from Parking Mirador de Ubaba, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail leading through forest and open areas, often completed in about 2 hours 8 minutes. Hiking around Monte Común De Las Améscoas is defined by karst formations, dense beech forests, and mountain pastures. The network of hiking trails offers options for various ability levels, from moderate forest walks to longer routes with elevation changes. The routes in Monte Común De Las Améscoas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 460 reviews. More than 3000 hikers have used komoot to explore Monte Común De Las Améscoas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Monte Común De Las Améscoas?
A: Monte Común De Las Améscoas offers a wide selection of routes, with over 200 hiking trails to explore. These range from easy walks to more challenging treks, ensuring options for various fitness levels.

Q: What makes hiking in Monte Común De Las Améscoas unique?
A: The region is characterized by its distinctive karst topography, featuring impressive cliffs and ravines, alongside vast beech forests and open mountain pastures. A major highlight is the breathtaking &lt;strong&gt;Nacedero del Urederra&lt;/strong&gt;, the source of the Urederra River, known for its turquoise pools and cascading waterfalls. The diverse climate also supports a rich variety of flora and fauna.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of hiking trails in Monte Común De Las Améscoas?
A: The trails in Monte Común De Las Améscoas cater to a range of abilities. You'll find approximately 25 easy routes, 143 moderate trails, and 42 more difficult options. This variety allows hikers to choose paths that match their experience and desired challenge.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking routes in Monte Común De Las Améscoas?
A: Yes, there are several family-friendly options. Many of the easier and moderate trails are suitable for families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20510758" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ubaba Viewpoint – Arteko Saro Dolmen loop from Parking Mirador de Ubaba&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8 km trail that takes about 2 hours 8 minutes, offering forest and open areas, which can be enjoyable for families.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Monte Común De Las Améscoas?
A: Yes, many trails in the region are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966165831/cave-of-the-cristinos-hermitage-of-san-benito-circular-from-larraona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Cristinos - Hermitage of San Benito – circular from Larraona&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 6.3 km path that explores varied terrain and interesting landmarks.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the hiking trails?
A: Hikers can discover a wealth of natural attractions. The iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1390836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Urederra River&lt;/a&gt; with its stunning waterfalls and turquoise pools is a must-see. You can also visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3065525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portupekoleze Arch&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3006763" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Los Cristinos&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3080140" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tobería Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;. The region's karst landscape also features unique rock formations and viewpoints like Larregoiko Peak.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Monte Común De Las Améscoas?
A: The region is beautiful year-round, but spring and autumn are particularly recommended. In spring, the forests are lush and green, and the waterfalls are at their fullest. Autumn offers spectacular displays of color as the beech leaves change, especially in areas like the 'Enchanted Forest of Artea'.

Q: Are there any trails that offer panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. The region is home to several viewpoints that provide stunning vistas. Larregoiko Peak, at 1,019 meters, offers privileged natural vantage points with splendid panoramic views of the Améscoas villages and the surrounding Urbasa mountain range. The &lt;strong&gt;El Balcón de Ubaba&lt;/strong&gt; (Urbasa Viewpoint) is also renowned for its wide-ranging views and excellent birdwatching opportunities.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Monte Común De Las Améscoas?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 460 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse natural landscapes, the stunning beauty of the Nacedero del Urederra, the tranquility of the beech forests, and the well-maintained network of trails suitable for various levels.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the hiking areas?
A: While public transport options directly into the heart of the Urbasa-Andía Natural Park and Monte Común De Las Améscoas are limited, local bus services may connect to nearby towns. It is generally recommended to use a car for more flexible access to trailheads and attractions within the park.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails?
A: Designated parking areas are typically available near popular trailheads and key attractions within the Urbasa-Andía Natural Park. For instance, there is parking available for routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20510758" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ubaba Viewpoint – Arteko Saro Dolmen loop from Parking Mirador de Ubaba&lt;/a&gt;. It's advisable to check specific route details for parking information before your hike.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites to explore while hiking?
A: Yes, the region has a rich history. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6195223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iranzu Monastery&lt;/a&gt;, a significant religious building nearby. Additionally, the area contains vestiges of ancient human presence, including an important megalithic station, adding a historical dimension to your explorations.
tips: A place that you cannot miss visiting if you are in the Urbasa-Andia Natural Park. You will have the best panoramic views of the entire Park and if you stay with little taste you can extend your route to reach the waterfall and the source of the Urredera River, another wonderful place.

One of the several waterfalls on the Urredera riverbed and an unmissable place for any nature lover. The environment and landscape that surround it are wonderful and perfectly complement this beautiful waterfall.
The route to the source of the Urederra river is highly directed due to erosion and the fragility of the landscape, so it is essential to respect it in order to preserve it.
